response.justification: Bogdan is a capable and broadly experienced engineer, but his background is almost entirely TypeScript, Node.js, and D language — not Java or Kotlin. The role requires deep JVM expertise, strong Spring ecosystem knowledge, and genuine experience with enterprise Java frameworks, none of which are meaningfully evidenced in the resume. Java is listed in skills and appeared peripherally at National Instruments and Altom, but at no depth that approaches the JVM internals and enterprise framework mastery this position demands. His IDE tooling work at NI is interesting but was in C for a C IDE, not JVM-based tooling.
strengths: Demonstrated experience building developer tooling and language-adjacent infrastructure (MCP code generators, test runners, assertion libraries)
Strong large-scale systems background including high-throughput micro-services at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second
History of contributions to IDE-adjacent work (NI LabWindows CVI C IDE, CI/CD infrastructure, build tooling)
concerns: No Kotlin experience whatsoever — a core required qualification for this role
No evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience
Primary engineering identity is TypeScript/Node.js and D language, not Java/JVM — Java appears only in a peripheral, non-deep capacity
